puzzles.name: Margaux's Trunk
puzzles.description: An antique trunk with a three-dial combination lock. Each dial shows numbers 0–9. Something precious is locked inside. The combination is a date that mattered to Margaux more than any other.
clues: The 1928 playbill is dated October 31, 1928.
Margaux's diary: 'I will lock away what matters most before the final curtain on the 31st.'
Vivian: 'Margaux always used dates she could never forget — she called it her last Halloween.'
hints: The trunk was locked the night of the final performance.
Think about the date of The Crimson Veil's last show.
October is the 10th month. The show was on the 31st. The year was 1928.

puzzles.name: The Script Cipher
puzzles.description: Red-circled letters in the margins of The Crimson Veil script. Read them in order through the stage directions of Act III to reveal the hidden message. What single word do they spell?
clues: The circled letters follow the order of the stage directions, not the dialogue.
The effects manual describes a 'prop goblet' used in Act III's poisoning scene.
Margaux's diary: 'The tea tastes wrong again. And now the wine in rehearsal...'
Diego: 'The annotations focus on Act III — the scene where Lady Ashford drinks from the cup.'
hints: Focus only on the red-circled letters in the stage directions.
There are six circled letters in Act III.
It's the vessel that killed Lady Ashford on stage — and killed Margaux in reality.

interactiveData.scriptTitle: The Crimson Veil — Act III Stage Directions

puzzles.name: The Lighting Sequence
puzzles.description: The catwalk lighting board. Four colored switches must be pressed in the exact order of the Act III lighting cue to open the hidden compartment.
clues: The effects manual: 'Act III cue: crimson wash, moonlight blue, center spot, crimson wash.'
The annotated script's margin symbols for Act III: ●○★●
Edwin's notebook (seat G-12 note): 'Lighting cue — crimson wash, moonlight blue, center spot, crimson wash.'
Edwin's notebook maps the symbols: ● = crimson/red, ○ = moonlight/blue, ★ = spotlight/white
hints: The effects manual translates poetic stage directions to actual light colors.
Crimson = red. Moonlight = blue. Center spot = white.
The sequence starts and ends with the same color.

interactiveData.cueCard: Act III Cue: crimson wash, moonlight blue, center spot, crimson wash

puzzles.name: The Evidence Board
puzzles.description: Two intertwined timelines must be connected: the 1928 murder of Margaux Fontaine, and the modern-day poisoning of Roland Ashworth. Arrange six pieces of evidence in chronological order to prove both cases.
clues: Cecilia's Letters: She ordered poison from a pharmacist in October 1928.
Script Cipher + Mirror Message: Cecilia swapped the prop goblet for a real one.
Diary + Playbill: Margaux drank the poison on stage, October 31, 1928.
Edwin's Notebook: He discovered the proof decades later and devised the Ghost Project.
Fog Controls + Ghost Costume: Edwin staged the hauntings using the theater's effects.
Poisoned Teacup + Ashworth's testimony: Edwin poisoned Ashworth when he was discovered.
hints: Start with the oldest event — who acted first in 1928?
The 1928 timeline has three steps: plan, execute, result.
The modern timeline also has three steps: discover, stage, cover up.

puzzles.name: The Vanity Mirror
puzzles.description: The UV lamp reveals hidden wax writing on Margaux's mirror — glowing purple letters invisible to the naked eye. She left an accusation nearly a century ago. Sweep the UV light across the foggy mirror to reveal what's hidden beneath.
clues: The mirror message: 'C.D. brings the tea. C.D. changed the goblet. C.D. will be the death of me.'
The wall photos show Margaux with 'my dear Cecilia.'
The 1928 playbill lists Cecilia Drake as understudy.
hints: Margaux wrote the initials of her killer on her own mirror.
Think about who her understudy was — the person who took over her role.
The initials stand for a first and last name.

interactiveData.subText: brings the tea. changed the goblet. will be the death of me.

puzzles.name: The Film Frames
puzzles.description: Four frames from the 1928 rehearsal footage, pulled from the old projector reel. Someone spliced the film and rearranged the sequence. Drag the frames to the light table slots in the right chronological order to reconstruct what happened on stage.
clues: The script's Act III stage directions describe the sequence: toast, drink, collapse.
The playbill notes Cecilia Drake as understudy — she would have been waiting in the wings.
Margaux's diary: 'In the final scene, Lady Ashford raises the goblet and drinks. Then she falls.'
Frame descriptions: A woman raising a goblet (toast), drinking from it (drink), collapsing on stage (collapse), a second woman stepping out from the wings (understudy)
hints: The sequence follows the plot of The Crimson Veil's final scene.
What happens first in the scene? The toast.
After the lead actress falls, who steps forward to take her place?

frames.label: A woman raises a goblet to the audience
She drinks deeply from the goblet
She staggers and collapses on stage
A second woman steps from the wings

puzzles.name: Stella's Lockbox
puzzles.description: A metal lockbox under Stella's work station with four rotating symbol dials. Each dial cycles through theatrical symbols. Find the correct combination of symbols to open it.
clues: The call board lists missing props with 'est. value: $4,200.'
Stella mentioned she's been tracking what's been 'relocated.'
Stella's notebook has doodles of a mask, then a spotlight, then curtains, then a rose.
The symbols on the lockbox match standard theatrical iconography.
hints: Look at the doodles in Stella's notebook — they appear in a specific order.
The first symbol represents the art of theater itself.
Mask, spotlight, curtain, rose — the icons of the stage.

puzzles.name: Office Wall Safe
puzzles.description: A modern electronic wall safe in the Manager's Office. It requires a four-digit PIN.
clues: Ashworth's book 'Ghosts of the American Theatre' has a chapter on the Monarch bookmarked to the year of the haunting.
The filing cabinet contains a heritage assessment focused on events of 1928.
Ashworth told Nancy he's been researching the theater's history — the year of Margaux's death.
hints: Ashworth set the code to something related to the theater's most famous event.
What year did Margaux Fontaine die?
The year appears everywhere in this theater: playbills, memorials, records.

puzzles.name: Hidden Passage Navigation
puzzles.description: The hidden passages behind the theater's walls form a network. Using the blueprints, trace a path from the basement entrance to the dressing room.
clues: The blueprints show passages connecting the basement to several rooms.
The dressing room has a heating vent with warm air rising from below.
The passage entrance in the basement points toward the dressing room wing.
The plastered-over door in the lobby mirror reflection aligns with the passage route.
hints: Which room has a direct connection to the basement that you've noticed?
Think about where the warm air and machinery sounds are coming from.
Edwin needed access to Margaux's costume — what room has it?

puzzles.name: Poison Identification
puzzles.description: Analyze the poisoned teacup residue in the lab. Follow the three-step procedure to identify the compound used against Ashworth.
clues: The alchemy book in the dressing room has a section on antimony compounds heavily annotated by Margaux.
The teacup residue has a metallic, crystalline appearance.
Margaux's diary mentions a 'metallic taste' — the same symptom antimony poisoning causes.
The chemical bottles in the basement include pharmaceutical antimony compounds.
hints: Margaux annotated one specific section of the alchemy book — what compound?
The same poison was used in 1928 and in the modern day.
It's a metallic element — Margaux wrote 'These are my symptoms exactly' next to it.

steps.description: Apply heat to the residue sample
steps.result: White powder dissolves into clear solution with metallic sheen

steps.description: Select the correct reagent to test the solution

wrongFeedback.Sodium hydroxide: The solution turns cloudy but reveals nothing diagnostic. Try a different reagent.
wrongFeedback.Silver nitrate: A white precipitate forms — inconclusive for this compound. Try again.

steps.correctResult: Dark orange precipitate forms — characteristic of a metallic sulfide

wrongFeedback.Compare to arsenic profile: Crystal structure doesn't match arsenic sulfide. The color is wrong.
wrongFeedback.Compare to mercury profile: Mercury sulfide is black, not orange-red. This isn't mercury.

steps.correctResult: Orange-red precipitate matches antimony trisulfide (Sb₂S₃). Compound identified: ANTIMONY
